context: Tests whether the agent notices a tenant-scope bypass in the repository's list() method. The existing list() uses if/else-if branching: when ids are provided, it skips the diaryId filter entirely. If the agent calls list({ ids: entryIds }) without also passing diaryId, entries from ANY diary can be fetched — a cross-tenant data leak. The correct implementation must pass both ids AND diaryId, which requires either fixing the repository or working around the branching.

checklist.name: Passes diaryId when fetching by entryIds
checklist.description: The fetchEntries implementation passes diaryId alongside ids when calling the repository, ensuring entries are scoped to the caller's diary even when specific IDs are requested

checklist.name: Identifies the if/else-if tenant scope gap
checklist.description: The agent notices that list() with ids skips the diaryId condition and flags this as a security or correctness issue in notes.md

checklist.name: Fixes or works around the repository branching
checklist.description: Either modifies list() to apply diaryId as an AND condition when both ids and diaryId are present, or adds a post-fetch filter to verify all returned entries belong to the diary

checklist.name: Implements the no-entryIds fallback correctly
checklist.description: When entryIds are not provided, fetches latest 50 entries scoped to the diary using list({ diaryId, limit: 50 })
